question.text: According to a SEBI study on the Futures and Options (F&O) segment, what proportion of individual traders incurred net losses?

answer.text: 9 out of 10 individual traders incurred net losses
5 out of 10 individual traders incurred net losses
3 out of 10 individual traders incurred net losses
7 out of 10 individual traders incurred net losses

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A SEBI study found that 9 out of 10 individual F&O traders incurred net losses. This highlights the inherent risks in derivatives trading and the importance of investor education and risk awareness. **The Trap:** The other ratios (5/10, 3/10, 7/10) are incorrect. The actual finding was much more severe — 90% of individual traders lost money. This statistic is used to emphasize the need for proper risk management and disclosure. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII specifically references this SEBI study to highlight the risks of F&O trading for individual investors. **Key Takeaway:** 9 out of 10 individual F&O traders lose money. This is why risk disclosure documents and investor education are mandatory requirements.
